title: [VPlan] Add hidden `-vplan-print-after-all` option
author: eas
description:
This consists of the following changes: 
        
* Merge several overloads of `VPlanTransforms::runPass` into a single function 
  to avoid code duplication.
  
* Add helper macro `RUN_VPLAN_PASS` to capture the transformation name
  and pass it to the helper above for printing.

* Add new `-vplan-print-after-all` option (somewhat similar to existing
  `-vplan-verify-each`).
  
 * Add two empty passes `printAfterInitialConstruction`/`printFinalVPlan` so that initial/final
   VPlans would be supported in `-vplan-print-after-all`

I believe this follows the original future plans in https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/123640.

tests.test_body:
define void @foo(ptr %ptr, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %header

header:                                           ; preds = %header, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %header ]
  %gep = getelementptr i64, ptr %ptr, i64 %iv
  store i64 %iv, ptr %gep, align 4
  %iv.next = add nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exitcond = icmp slt i64 %iv.next, %n
  br i1 %exitcond, label %header, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %header
  ret void
}

comments.author: eas
comments.body: @fhahn , @ayalz , does this make sense in general to you? This will be constantly resulting in merge conflicts so I'd like to avoid rebases until/unless general direction looks ok.

comments.author: eas
comments.body:
> Can you include a simple test in unittests/VPlanTest.cpp?

Not really, because the `LLVM_PRETTY_FUNCTION` can produce different outputs depending on the compiler used to build LLVM.

> it will be very useful to have this, thanks for working on this. just thinking if theres an alternative that avoids touching every invocation

I couldn't think of any. In fact, due to the instability of the produced name I'm thinking about making further [changes](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/commit/3b30c5c12c2e566968d0d7afb72c1367ba9868a1) that would touch those lines again (and would add a test now that it would be possible).

What I'd like to enable is to be able to write

```
// RUN: opt --pases=loop-vectorize -vplan-print-after=<VPlanTransformation> -disable-output < %s 2>&1 | FileCheck %s
...
```

so that we could verify the changes in a more direct way. However, because `LLVM_PRETTY_FUNCTION` isn't stable enough to be used in tests I have to write the names of the transformations explicitly. @fhahn , do you think those future changes should be kept separate or pulled into this PR?

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body:
> > Can you include a simple test in unittests/VPlanTest.cpp?
> 
> Not really, because the `LLVM_PRETTY_FUNCTION` can produce different outputs depending on the compiler used to build LLVM.
> 
Ah I see that's a shame. Do you know what's causing the differences?

comments.author: eas
comments.body:
> > > Can you include a simple test in unittests/VPlanTest.cpp?
> > 
> > 
> > Not really, because the `LLVM_PRETTY_FUNCTION` can produce different outputs depending on the compiler used to build LLVM.
> 
> Ah I see that's a shame. Do you know what's causing the differences?

What do you mean? First, it can be just `__func__`:

https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/9c2124e0f6bb94a9ce4eafe99f62ed0bf61833fe/llvm/include/llvm/Support/Compiler.h#L667-L678

which would have no information about the pass being executed (https://godbolt.org/z/eGY3Gcnob) thus making it unusable for our purposes. 

Second, even for gcc/clang/MSVC it would differ in how the name is printed and we can only reverse engineer it to extract the pass name from it, from this PR: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/e9fcabd7dd53316f2711d7a736eece7a91406dac/llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanTransforms.h#L51-L61

which would always be best efforts but not guaranteed.

comments.author: eas
comments.body:
> Could we split this PR into two? One that just changes runPass to print afterwards, and then in a separate one we could look at migrating other transforms to use runPass.

Changed this PR to only modify existing usage of the `runPass` helper + use macro to get the name. Now that name is stable added a test as well.

comments.author: eas
comments.body:
> LGTM, just a minor comment about the TODO left behind

Thanks! I'll need help merging this once enough approvals are given.

comments.author: eas
comments.body:
> Would great if you could double check and if possible share that patch as well just to make sure everything fits together nicely.

Branch: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/compare/main...eas:llvm-project:add-vplan-print-after
Individual changes:
`-vplan-print-after`: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/commit/c806a378de7f91c0b6a0b34efba6f94fc0645476
Initial UTC support (no captures handling): https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/commit/ac6e2c72d21c5f04ae757805d9e16d5be9f5bab7

I think that's enough for proof-of-concept, would turn into proper PRs once this one is merged.

Your changes will be combined with recent changes from other authors, then tested by our [build bots](https://lab.llvm.org/buildbot/). If there is a problem with a build, you may receive a report in an email or a comment on this PR.

Please check whether problems have been caused by your change specifically, as the builds can include changes from many authors. It is not uncommon for your change to be included in a build that fails due to someone else's changes, or infrastructure issues.

How to do this, and the rest of the post-merge process, is covered in detail [here](https://llvm.org/docs/MyFirstTypoFix.html#myfirsttypofix-issues-after-landing-your-pr).

If your change does cause a problem, it may be reverted, or you can revert it yourself. This is a normal part of [LLVM development](https://llvm.org/docs/DeveloperPolicy.html#patch-reversion-policy). You can fix your changes and open a new PR to merge them again.

If you don't get any reports, no action is required from you. Your changes are working as expected, well done!

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body: @eas one other thing I played around with more isolated testing is a way to run only a subset of VPlan transforms, with a pass that allows specifying widening decisions via metadata, to have an easy way to construct a range of interesting VPlans via plain LLVM IR: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/178522

comments.author: eas
comments.body:
> @eas one other thing I played around with more isolated testing is a way to run only a subset of VPlan transforms, with a pass that allows specifying widening decisions via metadata, to have an easy way to construct a range of interesting VPlans via plain LLVM IR: #178522

You won't believe...

https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/compare/main...eas:llvm-project:predicator-idom-mask

In particular this commit: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/commit/29eb1e08d880de0528f2914466d00156a4e9768c

I went a step further and eliminated the loop. One can think of it as vectorizing a function with `#pragma omp sims vector-variants` annotation, but skipping artificial loop and generating a VPlan directly. I was planning to creating a PR to discuss that approach after my print-after*/UTC changes are merged in.
